title: Analyses Object
description: Provides access to the existing analysis results within a design.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: A method that returns the specified Analysis using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: A method that returns the specified Analysis using the name of the analysis as it is displayed in the browser.

properties.name: accessibilityAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the AccessibilityAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing AccessibilityAnalysis objects in the design.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of Analysis objects in the collection.

properties.name: curvatureCombAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the CurvatureCombAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing CurvatureCombAnalysis objects in the design.

properties.name: curvatureMapAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the CurvatureMapAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing CurvatureMapAnalysis objects in the design.

properties.name: draftAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the DraftAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing DraftAnalysis objects in the design.

properties.name: isLightBulbOn
properties.description: A property that gets and sets if the display is enabled for all Analysis objects in the design. If this is false, all Analysis results will be hidden. If this is true, the Analysis objects whose isLightBulbOn property is also true will be visible.

properties.name: isoCurveAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the IsoCurveAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing IsoCurveAnalysis objects in the design.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: minimumRadiusAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the MinimumRadiusAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing MinimumRadiusAnalysis objects in the design.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: sectionAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the SectionAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing SectionAnalysis objects in the design.

properties.name: zebraAnalyses
properties.description: Returns the ZebraAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing ZebraAnalysis objects in the design.

accessibilityAnalyses.title: Analyses.accessibilityAnalyses Property
accessibilityAnalyses.description: Returns the AccessibilityAnalyses object, which provides access to any existing AccessibilityAnalysis objects in the design.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = analyses_var.accessibilityAnalyses

count.title: Analyses.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of Analysis objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = analyses_var.count

isLightBulbOn.title: Analyses.isLightBulbOn Property
isLightBulbOn.description: A property that gets and sets if the display is enabled for all Analysis objects in the design. If this is false, all Analysis results will be hidden. If this is true, the Analysis objects whose isLightBulbOn property is also true will be visible.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = analyses_var.isLightBulbOn

# Set the value of the property.
analyses_var.isLightBulbOn = propertyValue

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: Analyses.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: Analyses.item Method
item.description: A method that returns the specified Analysis using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: Analyses.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: A method that returns the specified Analysis using the name of the analysis as it is displayed in the browser.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the Analysis as it is displayed in the browser.
